Fear lingered in the heavy air where Mr. Thorne had left years ago, taking the trust with him. She did not lecture, nor did she demand grades. Instead, she offered patience.

Mrs. Gable wrote words on the board—never names, never dates. She chose terms like brave and kind. She guided them to build new maps. Each step was taken with measured care. No one rushed the work.

Time passed slowly, yet confidence grew. A shy girl finally spoke up. Her voice carried strength. The entire class clapped softly. Hope filled the room. This shift marked a turning point in their lives. The darkness lifted away. The future looked bright again.<|im_end|>
